name: Lamb, Raisin, & Bean Polov <Pilaf>
recipeIngredient: 12 Dates, pitted and diced into 1/4-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
EGG CRUST DIRECTIONS:
1. Beat the eggs in a large bowl and mix with the rice. Set aside until needed.
2. POLOV DIRECTIONS:
- Soak the beans overnight, rinse well, and place in a large heavy pot. Add 3 cups of water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour, skimming often.
- Rinse the rice thoroughly, then place in a bowl. Cover with lukewarm water and 1 teaspoon salt; let soak for 1 hour. Drain the beans.
- Heat oil in a large skillet. Add meat, onions, and garlic; brown the meat and onions/garlic for about 10 minutes.
- Mix saffron with 2 tablespoons of warm water and add to the meat, seasoning with salt and pepper. Add stock, cinnamon, turmeric, nutmeg, etc., bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
- Heat butter in a small skillet over medium-low heat. Add raisins and dates; cook until softened. Remove from heat and set aside.
- Bring 3 quarts of water to a boil in a large pot with salt. Add rinsed rice, stirring for 8 minutes. Remove from heat and drain the rice. It will still be hard; rinse with cold water.
- Mix melted butter and warm water in a bowl and pour into a large heatproof casserole with a tight-fitting lid. Place the crust into the casserole, ensuring it covers the bottom completely.
- Spread 1 cup of rice over the crust, then layer meat, beans, dates, raisins, and repeat until all ingredients are used. Top with remaining rice and drizzle with 5 tablespoons of butter.
- Pour remaining stock over this layer of rice. Poke 6-7 holes in the top layer of rice with the handle of a wooden spoon.
- Wrap the casserole lid with linen or cotton cloth; avoid using terry cloth towels as they absorb steam. Place the lid tightly on the casserole and cook over medium-high heat for 5 minutes, then reduce heat to low and steam for 40 minutes.
- Stir remaining butter with saffron in a small bowl. Remove polov from heat; let stand for 5 minutes. Remove 1 cup of rice from the top layer and toss with saffron-butter mixture before replacing on the polov.
- Serve immediately.
NOTE: In other parts of Central Asia, this dish is made with red beans and can be prepared using beef, horse, camel, chicken, or venison instead of lamb. It is excellent regardless of the meat used.
